title: Albuterol and Ipratropium Oral Inhalation: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

Ask your pharmacist or check the manufacturer's patient information for a list of the ingredients. tell your doctor and pharmacist what prescription and nonprescription medications, vitamins, nutritional supplements and herbal products you are taking or plan to take while using albuterol and ipratropium inhalation. Your doctor may need to change the doses of your medications or monitor you carefully for side effects. 
. Tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking the following medications or have stopped taking them within the past two weeks: antidepressants [amitriptyline, amoxapine, clomipramine (Anafranil Â® ), desipramine (Norpramin Â® ), doxepin (Silenor Â® ), imipramine (Tofranil Â® ), nortriptyline (Pamelor Â® ), protriptyline (Vivactil Â® ), trimipramine (Surmontil Â® )] or monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors [isocarboxazid (Marplan Â® ), phenelzine (Nardil Â® ), tranylcypromine (Parnate Â® ), selegiline (Eldepryl Â® , Emsam Â® , Zelapar Â® )]. The following nonprescription products may interact with albuterol and ipratropium inhalation: medications for colds.
